# Questions in Livestream 266

Live Stream #266: Three Major Questions

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=534" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:54</a> | In Genesis 39, what was Potiphar's wife's social status relative to Joseph, and what rights did she have as lady of the house? | He said her standing came largely from being the wife of a very powerful official, which gave her significant household authority and made any liaison with a slave a serious social violation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=704" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:44</a> | What are your thoughts on Steven L. Anderson and his effect on the church? | He was sharply negative and said Anderson's teachings are harmful and heretical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=1327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:07</a> | In Luke 22:19-20, what does remembrance mean in context? | He said the Greek term means to remember or call back to mind, and that the text itself clearly includes the remembrance aspect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=1591" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:31</a> | In debates about the true church, does the church sometimes seem to say more about God's will than God does? | He warned that churches can turn institutional authority into an idol when they speak as though they replace God's own word. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=1825" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:25</a> | Is it a problem to call the Late Bronze IIB mudbrick wall at Jericho a casemate if the foundation was only two meters wide? | No. He said the surviving remains are incomplete, but there is evidence for casing walls and the missing interior wall may have been removed by site leveling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=1942" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:22</a> | How would you explain Christ's divinity in John 17:20-22 to Mormons and Muslims? | He said he would not start there, because Mormons redefine the unity language and Muslims generally reject the Bible's authority. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=2333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:53</a> | Did the Greeks and Romans try to convert Egypt from beer to wine? | No. He said Egypt had wine from very early periods, though it was mostly an upper-class drink while beer was more common. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=2455" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:55</a> | Can God's grace be applied to a person without sin? | Yes. He defined grace as unmerited favor, which can include blessings and opportunities that are not only about forgiving sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=2582" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:02</a> | Have you ever tried Scottish food? | Yes. He said he tried haggis while living in England, liked it, and would eat real Scottish haggis by choice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=2796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:36</a> | How important are the creeds of the Christian faith? | He said they are important doctrinal summaries and boundaries for interpretation, though they are not replacements for Scripture and translations can need care. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3036" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:36</a> | Can you win a Nobel Prize for Egyptology? | No. He said there is no Nobel category for history, Egyptology, or the liberal arts apart from literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:18</a> | Would you rather have an "actually, I'm the author" moment or a "trust me, I'm a doctor" moment? | He would rather have the author moment because it is more fun and less likely to make people shut down. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:25</a> | What do you think happened to the ark? | He thinks it was likely taken to Babylon and eventually burned or stripped for its gold. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:23</a> | If Babylon melted the ark, why did they not melt all the temple artifacts? | He said they probably melted what they needed to pay troops and kept some attractive vessels for later use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3437" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:17</a> | Would the Babylonians have died the moment they touched the ark? | No. He said the ark's lethal danger depended on the Shekinah glory being present, and by then the ark would have been inert. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3584" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:44</a> | If the Ark of the Covenant were found today, would it still have power? | No. He said it would have no special power apart from the departed Shekinah glory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:08</a> | Are you more fond of spaghetti than lasagna? | No. He strongly preferred lasagna and said it is a good test of an Italian restaurant's quality. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=3957" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:57</a> | In Matthew 13:1-9, does "whoever has ears, let them hear" reveal the split between those with faith and those without? | Not quite. He connected it more to understanding and God's grace than to a simple faith-versus-no-faith split. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=4160" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:20</a> | In John 12:27-30, was the heavenly voice a revelation of the split between believers and unbelievers? | He said it illustrates that even direct revelation is received differently depending on whether people have grace to hear it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=4362" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:42</a> | Does Acts 17:26 refute the idea of other humans before Adam? | No. He said the text is not specific enough because it says "one" without clearly saying one man, Adam, race, or nation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=4658" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:38</a> | Does Matthew 12:32 imply that some sins could be forgiven in the age to come? | He did not take it that way; he read it as emphasizing that blasphemy against the Spirit cuts a person off from the path to repentance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=4867" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:07</a> | Is apostasy the same thing as blasphemy against the Holy Spirit? | Yes. He identified apostasy with turning away from the faith and rejecting God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=4915" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:55</a> | What is blasphemy against the Holy Spirit? | He summarized it as treating the Holy Spirit as worthless and rejecting the Spirit's work of bringing a person to repentance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=5124" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:24</a> | Would houses built next to the mudbrick layer at Jericho get in the way of a casemate foundation? | Not really. He said ancient builders could fill or build over houses and place later walls on top. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=5237" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:17</a> | Are you saying they put mudbrick over the houses? | Yes. He said walls built over houses are known archaeologically and create reconstruction and conservation problems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=5408" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:08</a> | What spiritual gifts may someone possess besides speaking in tongues? | He listed gifts such as hospitality, prophecy, wisdom, discernment, interpretation of tongues, and teaching, all for the church's edification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=5469" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:09</a> | Is there reason to believe misuse of gifts bestowed by God will bring harsher judgment? | Yes. He said spiritual gifts are for building up the church, so misusing them for personal purposes would bring judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=5574" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:54</a> | Are talent as money and talent as ability related etymologically? | Yes. He said the ability sense comes from the monetary unit, which was a very large amount of precious metal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=5930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:50</a> | Did the Knights Templar invent banking? | No. He said money lending was much older; the Templars' innovation was closer to international banking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:56</a> | Have you read Hoffmeier's new book on Israel in and out of Egypt, and what do you think of it? | Not yet. He said he was still waiting to receive a review copy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6247" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:07</a> | Did Arab invaders intentionally try to exterminate Coptic Egyptians during the Islamic conquest? | He described it more as long-term assimilation through pressure, taxation, and family policy than immediate extermination. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6565" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:25</a> | What does the Canadian bill stop? | He said it removes the religious exemption for hate speech and exposes people to human-rights tribunal penalties. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6618" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:18</a> | Does that law extend into churches? | Yes. He said it does extend to churches. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:53</a> | Is YouTube Canada different from YouTube in the United States? | No. He said it is the same company and the same YouTube community guidelines apply. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6758" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:38</a> | Is Canada now worse than Australia in terms of government reach? | He said yes, much worse, because of the scope and penalties he described. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6773" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:53</a> | Is this applied equally to other religions as well? | He said no, and expected uneven application. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6815" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:35</a> | Is there a marginalization of more conservative Christian voices in biblical scholarship? | Yes. He said that especially happens in atheistic or very liberal parts of the field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=6886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:46</a> | Which Egyptian records from the Exodus era could count as methodologically serious indirect evidence for the plagues? | He said there really are none, largely because the relevant Delta records from Pi-Ramesses and Avaris were destroyed by salt groundwater. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=7378" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:58</a> | Can you describe my art, the scatterbug? | Yes. He described it as a small type-four scatterbug with a round body, one large eye, eight legs, front manipulators, and a hatch underneath. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=7935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:15</a> | Is there still more to discover at Jericho? | Absolutely. He said no site is ever really exhausted because archaeologists do not dig up an entire site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=7973" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:53</a> | What Christian-based movies does David like? | He named films such as *Hacksaw Ridge*, *Greyhound*, and *Silence* as films with Christian themes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=8127" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:27</a> | What is your favorite lesser-known genus or species of bird? | He said he is not really a bird person, but would have liked to see the extinct Bennu heron. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=8244" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:24</a> | How would you answer the claim that if something appears only in the New Testament, it is automatically wrong? | He called that a genetic fallacy and poor reasoning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=8289" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:09</a> | How does one acquire a grudge against birds? | He said it is easy if one's usual bird encounters are with seagulls, Canada geese, and similar nuisance birds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=8790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:30</a> | How exactly should religion be defined? | He said there are multiple definitions, but a useful functional definition is that religion answers ultimately unanswerable questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=9072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:12</a> | What would you say to a Jewish person who does not accept Jesus as Messiah because he did not fulfill all the prophecies? | He said not all prophecies were meant to be fulfilled at once and would point them to redemption prophecies such as Isaiah 53. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=9350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:50</a> | How many types of prophecies are there? | He said there are several, including consummation, apocalyptic, redemption, and restoration prophecies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=9450" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:30</a> | What was Hades in the context of the Bible, and was it like Sheol? | Yes. He said Hades is much closer to Sheol than to later popular ideas of hell, unless context narrows the term. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=9550" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:10</a> | Why is it difficult to convert Hindus when their documents are legends? | He said the main obstacle is Hinduism's polytheistic and cultural framework, which can add Jesus without making him exclusive Lord. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=9888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:48</a> | Is YouTube's war on ad blockers legal? | Yes. He said YouTube can enforce it under its terms of service and that ad blockers hurt YouTubers' revenue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=10245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:45</a> | Will pseudoscience still be present in heaven? | No. He said pseudoscience depends on lies, and there are no lies in heaven. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=10286" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:26</a> | What does David think the One Piece is in *One Piece*? | He did not know because he has not watched much of the series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=10322" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:02</a> | Did Adam and Eve live in the Early Bronze Age? | No. He said that would be much too late and placed them far back in the Stone Age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=10365" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:45</a> | Are you aware of Emma Brunner-Traut's distinction between aspect and perspective, and what do you think of it? | Yes. He said there is something to it, but he thinks the theory has weaknesses and he does not work much with that kind of theoretical iconography. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=10603" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:43</a> | Is there a difference between fundamentalist scholarship and conservative Christian scholarship? | Yes. He said the difference comes down largely to hermeneutics: wooden literalism versus the grammatical-historical method. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=11148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:48</a> | Do any papyri show a husband and wife both physically present for a Roman census at the same time? | No. He said that is because census documents were ephemeral and generally have not survived. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=11391" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:51</a> | When Israelite kings did a census, was it part of the same process? | Yes. He connected Israelite royal censuses to taxation and corvee labor, especially under Solomon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=11530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:10</a> | When during Ramesses II's reign would the Exodus have happened? | He placed it around the king's twenty-fifth regnal year, roughly 1263 BC, with some room on either side. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=11567" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:47</a> | What is a proper interpretation of Revelation 13:8-10 regarding predestination? | He preferred attaching "from the foundation of the world" to the names in the book of life and said the passage can be read with either single or double predestination depending on soteriology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=12033" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:33</a> | Could you draw sketches of the robots, ships, and housing system at Gleise? | He showed the existing colony image, described the domes, and said robot and ship illustrations may come later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=12513" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:33</a> | Any more sleepless nights? | Yes. He said he is an insomniac and that is one reason he gets so much writing done. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=12861" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:21</a> | What is a chibi? | He described chibi as a childlike art style with a large head and a small body. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=12933" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:33</a> | Is there any chance of an ancient Greek or Japanese translation of the War God series? | Not realistically. He said translation would be expensive with a limited market. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=13056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:36</a> | Does Acts 15:29 forbid Christians from eating raw meat? | No. He said the passage concerns blood and strangled meat, not rare meat or steak tartare. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=13239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:39</a> | What are your thoughts on someone being both Christian and a Freemason? | He said Christianity and Freemasonry are mutually exclusive because Freemasonry treats Christianity as one truth among many and tends toward Gnosticism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=13549" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:49</a> | What are your thoughts on the Nebel pyramid? | He said the identification is debatable and offered only a cautious speculative reading, leaning toward a queen's pyramid with later opportunistic storage of cult equipment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=14002" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:22</a> | How do you balance your personal interests with God, especially pop culture interests? | He said to integrate them through a Christian worldview, pursue excellence in the craft, and avoid preachiness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=15251" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:14:11</a> | Was Pierce's training process in your fiction inspired by training at Walmart? | Indirectly, perhaps. He said he has worked in large retail and other workplaces, and those experiences influenced parts of the fiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=15454" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:17:34</a> | Why does Starbucks always burn their beans? | He said it is a way to mask low-quality beans, based on his experience working in a Starbucks bean processing plant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=15479" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:17:59</a> | Which pharaoh from each dynasty was the most evil? | He declined to answer because covering all thirty-two dynasties would take too long. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=15620" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:20:20</a> | How often do you get to do art, and in what medium? | He said not as much as he used to, and mentioned colored pencils, pastels, ink, watercolor, acrylic, oils, carving, wax work, casting, and lapidary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3lcFL2Atyps?t=16225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:30:25</a> | Have you heard of the indie game *Fresco*? | No. He had not heard of it but said it sounded interesting. |
